basics.name: Richard Hendriks
basics.label: Programmer
basics.summary: Richard hails from Tulsa. He has earned degrees from the University of Oklahoma and Stanford. (Go Sooners and Cardinal!) Before starting Pied Piper, he worked for Hooli as a part time software developer. While his work focuses on applied information theory, mostly optimizing lossless compression schema of both the length-limited and adaptive variants, his non-work interests range widely, everything from quantum computing to chaos theory. He could tell you about it, but THAT would NOT be a “length-limited” conversation!

work.name: Stripe
work.description: Financial infrastructure platform for the internet
work.summary: Led architecture for high-throughput payment processing pipelines, mentoring senior engineers and guiding technical strategy across multiple product teams.
highlights: Architected a new distributed ledger system that increased transaction throughput by 300%
Mentored a team of 15 engineers across 3 distinct product verticals
Spearheaded the migration to a modern microservices architecture built on Go and gRPC, reducing system latency by 40ms

work.name: Stripe
work.description: Financial infrastructure platform for the internet
work.summary: Core engineer on the billing API team, scaling systems to handle millions of requests per minute.
highlights: Designed and built the core fraud-detection ML pipeline, saving the company $5M+ per year
Optimized PostgreSQL queries, dropping P99 API latency from 200ms to 45ms
Led cross-functional initiatives to improve developer experience and CI/CD stability

work.name: Twitch
work.summary: Developed scalable backend services for live video delivery.
highlights: Improved video transcoding pipeline efficiency by 25%
Implemented real-time chat moderation features using machine learning
Scaled websocket connections to support 1M+ concurrent users per channel

work.name: Pied Piper
work.summary: Pied Piper is a multi-platform technology based on a proprietary universal compression algorithm that has consistently fielded high Weisman Scores™ that are not merely competitive, but approach the theoretical limit of lossless compression.
highlights: Build an algorithm for artist to detect if their music was violating copy right infringement laws
Successfully won Techcrunch Disrupt
Optimized an algorithm that holds the current world record for Weisman Scores

work.name: Hooli
work.summary: Lead developer for Hooli Search core algorithms.
highlights: Redesigned the entire search indexing pipeline, reducing indexing time by 40%
Managed a team of 4 junior developers and oversaw their technical growth
Patented a new approach to distributed key-value store optimization

work.name: Hooli
work.summary: Developed consumer-facing applications and optimized backend services for the Hooli platform.
highlights: Implemented real-time messaging features used by millions of DAUs
Optimized memory management in C++ modules, reducing segmentation faults by 80%
Researched middle-out compression algorithms in spare time

work.name: Innotech
work.summary: Started career building internal tools and squashing bugs for a legacy CRM platform.
highlights: Migrated 50+ legacy PHP components to modern React/Node.js stack
Wrote comprehensive unit tests, improving code coverage from 12% to 65%
Automated deployment pipelines to save 10 hours of manual work weekly

volunteer.summary: Global movement of free coding clubs for young people.
highlights: Awarded 'Teacher of the Month'

publications.name: Video compression for 3d media
publications.summary: Innovative middle-out compression algorithm that changes the way we store data.

references.name: Erlich Bachman
references.reference: It is my pleasure to recommend Richard, his performance working as a consultant for Main St. Company proved that he will be a valuable addition to any company.

projects.name: Miss Direction
projects.description: A mapping engine that misguides you
highlights: Won award at AIHacks 2016
Built by all women team of newbie programmers
Using modern technologies such as GoogleMaps, Chrome Extension and Javascript
